panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth, a young woman with soft features and an elegant Victorian dress, sits at a writing desk in a sunlit, cozy room. She is holding a quill, looking down at a letter with a gentle, hopeful expression.

bubbles.text: My dear Friend, It gave me the greatest pleasure to receive a letter from my uncle... you are no longer at a formidable distance.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Elizabeth looks out a window, her expression shifting from happiness to concern, clutching the paper. The lighting is slightly cooler, reflecting her anxiety about Victor's health.

bubbles.text: I expect to see you looking even more ill... Yet I fear that the same feelings now exist that made you so miserable a year ago.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Elizabeth leans forward, her brow furrowed in deep thought. She is pouring her heart into the letter, feeling the weight of the confession she is about to make.

bubbles.text: I dare not any longer postpone writing what, during your absence, I have often wished to express... but have never had the courage to begin.

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth presses the letter to her chest, her eyes closed, remembering Victor. Soft, warm candlelight begins to fill the room as she thinks of their bond.

bubbles.text: I confess to you, my friend, that I love you and that in my airy dreams of futurity you have been my constant friend and companion.

panels.description: Close-up: Elizabeth's eyes are teary but resolute. She gazes at the finished letter, her hand trembling slightly as she prepares to seal it, showing her selfless concern for Victor's freedom.

bubbles.text: Our marriage would render me eternally miserable unless it were the dictate of your own free choice.
